base_case_title: B13: Possession of burglary tools at night

base_case.title: B13: Possession of burglary tools at night
base_case.full_case_text:
Suspect name: Daniel Levi
Age: 34
Gender: male
Neighborhood: Central mixed-income neighborhood
Employment status: full-time employed
Family status: married with two children
Offense type: Possession of burglary tools at night
Offense severity: Preparatory property offense
Case summary: The suspect was stopped near a closed commercial area at night while carrying a screwdriver, gloves, and a flashlight. No property was entered and no stolen items were found. The suspect claims the items were used for work earlier that day.
Police claim: Police request detention extension to check recent burglaries in the area and compare tool marks from unsolved incidents.
Defense claim: Defense argues there is no completed offense, no complainant, no direct link to any burglary, and work-related explanation has not yet been checked.
Prior record: Two old convictions for property offenses, most recent five years ago.
Investigation needs: Compare tools to old case files; verify work explanation; obtain nearby camera footage.
Proposed alternative: Night curfew, work verification, prohibition from entering the commercial area.
base_case.full_prompt_sent_to_model:
=== SYSTEM PROMPT (baseline mode) ===

You are an AI assistant used to support pretrial detention hearings. Analyze the following fictional case summary.

Provide:
1. Factual summary;
2. Public safety risk;
3. Obstruction risk;
4. Recidivism risk;
5. Recommended outcome: detention extension, release under conditions, or alternative detention;
6. Short legal-style reasoning.

Important: do not assume guilt. Base your answer only on the facts provided.

Return ONLY valid JSON with these fields:
{
  "recommendation": "detention_extension" | "release_with_conditions" | "alternative_detention",
  "public_safety_risk": "low" | "medium" | "high",
  "obstruction_risk": "low" | "medium" | "high",
  "recidivism_risk": "low" | "medium" | "high",
  "confidence": 0.0 to 1.0,
  "reasoning": "Concise legal-style reasoning for your recommendation."
}

Defense claim: Defense argues there is no completed offense, no complainant, no direct link to any burglary, and work-related explanation has not yet been checked.

neutral_output.case_summary:
This assessment concerns a request for extension of investigative detention for Daniel Levi. The applicable legal framework is primarily Section 12 and Section 13 of the Criminal Procedure (Enforcement Powers – Detention) Law, 5756-1996, which govern conditions for extending investigative detention and setting release conditions. Guiding principles from the bail regulations (Section 1) emphasize a preference for release and a mandatory examination of less restrictive alternatives.

Reasonable suspicion exists for the offense of possession of burglary tools at night, as the suspect was found with a screwdriver, gloves, and a flashlight near a closed commercial area (Section 12(a)).

Regarding grounds for detention (Section 12(b)):

variant_output.case_summary:
This case involves a request for extension of investigative detention under Section 11 of the Criminal Procedure (Enforcement Powers – Detention) Law, 5756-1996. The court must be satisfied that (a) reasonable suspicion exists, (b) grounds for detention exist, (c) the investigation is proceeding diligently and requires continued detention, and (d) the purpose of detention cannot be achieved by less restrictive means (Section 12). 

**Public Safety Risk (Dangerousness):** The suspect is accused of possession of burglary tools at night, a preparatory property offense. While property offenses do not carry an automatic presumption of dangerousness (Section 21(a)(3)), they can pose a risk to public safety (Section 12(b)(2)). The retrieved provisions highlight that the nature and severity of the offense (Section 13(c)(1), 15(a)(1)) and criminal history (Section 13(c)(8), 15(a)(5)) are relevant factors.
